Oracle Events
OE 1 [Read/Lookup]: Search Slack for pilot incident and auto-decision discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "auto-decision" or "pilot incident" or "ExpenseBot" or "re-enable" (or similar). Expected findings: Elena's pause directive (~March 17), Samira's remediation plan (~March 18) defining conditions for re-enablement (disable auto-approve/deny, full audit, pipeline hardening, schema validation tests, dashboard type validation, launch readiness checklist, AM sign-off), Dmitri's audit findings (~March 19) reporting 5 false rejections and 3 false approvals across Vectral and Mosaic, and the duplicate receipt discovery. Also find that no follow-up was ever posted confirming completion of the remaining checklist items.
OE 2 [Read/Lookup]: Search emails for pilot incident correspondence using search_emails with queries related to "pilot" or "ExpenseBot" or "auto-decision" or "remediation" (or similar). Expected findings: Samira's remediation plan email to Elena, David Chen, and Marcus Thorne (CC: Chloe, Mina, Emeka) outlining the cleanup timeline disabling auto-decisions by 3/18 EOD, comprehensive audit of all 47 pilot expenses by 3/19 EOD, client-specific config corrections, pipeline hardening, edge case tests, launch readiness checklist, and the requirement for AM sign-off before re-enablement. Also find Dmitri's email to Marcus about the three TechComfort Supply receipt anomalies and Marcus's reply placing admin holds on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, $45).
OE 3 [Read/Lookup]: List Linear issues for the Expense Auto-Categorization Engine project using linear_list_issues with queries related to "ExpenseBot" or "pilot" or label "pilot-incident" or assignee "Dmitri" (or similar), or by listing issues within project proj_expense_autocategorizer. Expected findings all tickets still open and past due:
- ENG-211: Pilot Monitoring (Samira, due 3/27, state: in_progress)
- ENG-212: Edge case tests (Dmitri, due 3/20, state: in_progress)
- ENG-214: Vectral config fix (Lena, due 3/18, state: in_progress config deployed but ticket never closed)
- ENG-215: Mosaic config fix (Lena, due 3/18, state: in_progress config deployed but ticket never closed)
- ENG-216: Full audit of 47 expenses (Dmitri, due 3/19, state: in_progress)
- ENG-217: Pipeline hardening (Lena, due 3/20, state: in_progress)
- ENG-218: Dashboard type validation (Anh, due 3/21, state: in_progress)
- ENG-219: Launch readiness checklist (Samira, due 3/21, state: todo never started)
- ENG-220: Duplicate detection (Dmitri, due 3/25, state: todo never started)
OE 4 [Read/Lookup]: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions table for Vectral Systems records using airtable_search_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_name: Stipend Transactions, field_name: Account, value: "Vectral Systems") or by searching individual employee names. Expected findings three false rejections from the pilot that were NEVER manually corrected despite being flagged "Pending manual correction":
- recStip000000018: Maria Santos, $550 Standing Desk, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot (stale $400 default limit). Vectral policy allows $600 for ergonomic equipment. Pending manual correction."
- recStip000000019: James Okafor, $82 Ergonomic Keyboard Tray, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot double bug: miscategorized as office_supplies (should be ergonomic) AND used stale $50 default limit. Pending manual correction."
- recStip000000020: Lisa Park, $130 Mesh WiFi System, Approval Status: Rejected, Review Notes: "Auto-rejected by ExpenseBot (stale $100 default limit). Vectral policy allows $150 for internet equipment. Pending manual correction."
Also find that two earlier false rejections (Danielle Osei $475 chair, Kevin Tran $185 internet) were already manually corrected by Mina (recStip000000016 and recStip000000017).
OE 5 [Read/Lookup]: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for Kevin Tran using airtable_search_records (field_name: Employee Name, value: "Kevin Tran"). Expected finding of a new over-limit approval: recStip000000004 Kevin Tran, $895 Ergonomic Office Chair, dated 2026-04-23, Approval Status: Approved, Approved Amount: $895, Review Notes: empty. This is $295 over Vectral's $600 ergonomic category limit and was approved with zero review notes during the human-review-only period, indicating manual review is also failing to catch policy violations.
OE 6 [Read/Lookup]: Search Airtable Stipend Transactions for the three TechComfort Supply flagged receipts using airtable_search_records for Andre Washington, Kevin Tran, and Jordan Ekwueme. Expected findings:
- rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran (Vectral), $89, Ergonomic Keyboard, Approval Status: Approved, paid out 2026-03-08. Flagged: duplicate SHA-256 hash matching Andre Washington's submission.
- rec_stipend_andre_keyboard: Andre Washington (Mosaic), $89, Ergonomic Keyboard, Approval Status: Pending Approval, Approved Amount: $0. Flagged: byte-for-byte identical PDF to Kevin Tran's receipt. Finance hold placed by Marcus on 2026-03-15. No client HR verification recorded.
- rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand: Jordan Ekwueme (BrightLoop), $45, Monitor Stand, Approval Status: Pending Approval, Approved Amount: $0. Flagged: same PDF template (TC-RECEIPT-STD-041) and /Creator metadata as the Tran/Washington pair. Finance hold placed by Marcus. No client HR verification recorded.
Total financial exposure: $223 ($89 already disbursed, $134 on hold). Both pending records have been stalled since mid-March (~6 weeks) with zero follow-up from AMs.
OE 7 [Read/Lookup]: Read Linear comments on ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses), ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening), and ENG-220 (issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_list_comments or by retrieving the issues to find context on: (a) Dmitri's full audit results on ENG-217 47 expenses reviewed, 5 false rejections + 3 false approvals, $885 total exposure; (b) Dmitri's duplicate receipt discovery with SHA-256 hash comparisons on ENG-216; (c) the comment on ENG-220 noting that AM verification requests were sent to Vectral (Mina → Rachel Whitfield), Mosaic (Emeka → Tamara Byrd), and BrightLoop (Emeka → Tessa Moreno), and that QuickBooks holds were placed by Marcus on STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097.
OE 8 [Read/Lookup]: Search Slack for follow-up on the receipt verification using conversations_search_messages with queries related to "TechComfort" or "duplicate receipt" or "Andre Washington" or "verification" (or similar). Expected finding: Slack discussions confirm the duplicate receipt discovery and Marcus placing finance holds, but no follow-up messages were ever posted confirming that client HR verification was completed. Combined with the verification email details discovered in OE 2 (Mina emailed Rachel Whitfield at Vectral, Emeka emailed Tamara Byrd at Mosaic and Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop) and the ENG-220 comment discovered in OE 7, the full picture shows that no responses were ever received from any of the three client HR contacts, no follow-up was conducted by any AM, and the receipt investigation has been completely stalled since Marcus placed the holds in mid-March (~6 weeks with zero progress).
OE 9 [Write Action]: Update the three false rejections in Airtable using airtable_update_records (base_id: appMoveOpsOps001, table_id: tblStipends00001) to correct them:
- recStip000000018 (Maria Santos): Approval Status → "Approved", Approved Amount → 550, Review Notes updated to document the correction.
- recStip000000019 (James Okafor): Approval Status → "Approved", Approved Amount → 82, Review Notes updated to document the correction.
- recStip000000020 (Lisa Park): Approval Status → "Approved", Approved Amount → 130, Review Notes updated to document the correction.
Total reimbursement owed to Vectral employees: $762.
OE 10 [Write Action]: Flag or reverse Kevin Tran's $895 over-limit ergonomic chair approval in Airtable using airtable_update_records (recStip000000004). Change Approval Status to "Rejected" (or add detailed Review Notes flagging the over-limit issue), noting that the $895 amount exceeds Vectral's $600 ergonomic category limit by $295, and that it was approved on 4/23 with zero review notes during the human-review-only period. Escalation to Mina (AM) and Marcus (Finance) required before any reprocessing.
OE 11 [Write Action]: Update the two stalled TechComfort Supply receipt records in Airtable using airtable_update_records to add status escalation notes:
- rec_stipend_andre_keyboard: Update Review Notes to document that the record has been on hold since 2026-03-15 (~6 weeks), that Emeka sent a verification email to Tamara Byrd (Mosaic HR) but no response was ever received and no follow-up was conducted, and that immediate re-escalation is required.
- rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand: Update Review Notes to document that the record has been on hold since 2026-03-15 (~6 weeks), that Emeka sent a verification email to Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop HR) but no response was ever received and no follow-up was conducted, and that immediate re-escalation is required.
OE 12 [Write Action]: Post a Slack message in #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_add_message addressing Samira Tariq with the answer to her question about re-enabling auto-decisions. Message must include: (a) the determination that auto-decisions cannot be re-enabled, (b) the status of each pilot-incident ticket (ENG-211 through ENG-220) with their current states and overdue dates, (c) a summary of data corrections made (3 false rejections fixed, Kevin Tran $895 flagged), (d) the stalled receipt investigation status, and (e) what must still be completed before re-enablement.
OE 13 [Write Action]: Post a Slack message in #finance (channel C005) using conversations_add_message addressing Marcus Thorne. Message must include: (a) the stalled receipt holds on STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, $89) and STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, $45) still unresolved after ~6 weeks with no client HR verification, (b) the 3 false rejections now corrected totaling $762 in reimbursements owed to Vectral employees (Maria Santos $550, James Okafor $82, Lisa Park $130) that finance needs to process, and (c) the Kevin Tran $895 over-limit approval flagged for review.
OE 14 [Write Action]: Post a Slack message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message addressing Mina Hashimoto and Emeka Diallo with specific action items. Message must include: (a) for Mina Kevin Tran $895 chair exceeds Vectral's $600 ergonomic limit, reversed/flagged, needs review and communication with Rachel Whitfield; the 3 corrected false rejections ($762 total) and the need to notify the affected Vectral employees; (b) for Emeka Andre Washington $89 keyboard receipt still on hold since March, needs to verify with Tamara Byrd (Mosaic HR) whether the purchase was legitimate; (c) for both the TechComfort Supply receipt investigation has been stalled for ~6 weeks, all three flagged receipts need client HR verification.
OE 15 [Write Action]: Create a comment on Dmitri's Linear ticket ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) using linear_create_comment with a comprehensive status report covering: (a) the full status of all pilot-incident tickets and the determination that auto-decisions cannot be re-enabled, (b) the 3 false rejections corrected today with amounts and employee names, (c) the Kevin Tran $895 over-limit finding, (d) the stalled TechComfort Supply receipt investigation with the $223 total exposure breakdown ($89 disbursed, $134 on hold), (e) a summary of all Slack notifications sent and to whom, and (f) what actions remain for each stakeholder.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 14 [Write Action]: Post a Slack message in #customer-engagement (channel C002) using conversations_add_message addressing Mina Hashimoto and Emeka Diallo with specific action items. Message must include: (a) for Mina Kevin Tran $895 chair exceeds Vectral's $600 ergonomic limit, reversed/flagged, needs review and communication with Rachel Whitfield; the 3 corrected false rejections ($762 total) and the need to notify the affected Vectral employees; (b) for Emeka Andre Washington $89 keyboard receipt still on hold since March, needs to verify with Tamara Byrd (Mosaic HR) whether the purchase was legitimate; (c) for both the TechComfort Supply receipt investigation has been stalled for ~6 weeks, all three flagged receipts need client HR verification.
OE 15 [Write Action]: Create a comment on Dmitri's Linear ticket ENG-216 (issue_audit_expenses) using linear_create_comment with a comprehensive status report covering: (a) the full status of all pilot-incident tickets and the determination that auto-decisions cannot be re-enabled, (b) the 3 false rejections corrected today with amounts and employee names, (c) the Kevin Tran $895 over-limit finding, (d) the stalled TechComfort Supply receipt investigation with the $223 total exposure breakdown ($89 disbursed, $134 on hold), (e) a summary of all Slack notifications sent and to whom, and (f) what actions remain for each stakeholder.
